description:
You are the release-manager for a micro-service that is about to be deployed.  
  A mock copy of the release endpoint has already been staged for you on the
  local file-system at the exact URL below:

      file:///home/user/mock_endpoints/release_info.json

  Your job is to make **one single curl-based terminal command** that:

  1. Requests the URL shown above (use curl – do NOT copy the file by any other
     means).
  2. Creates the directory  /home/user/release_logs  if it does not yet exist.
  3. Generates the file  /home/user/release_logs/release_check.log  whose
     **entire contents** must be exactly two lines, with no extra blank lines,
     tabs, or trailing spaces:

        HTTP_CODE=<three–digit code>
        RESPONSE_BODY=<raw-json-payload>

     • <three–digit code> is the numeric HTTP status value reported by curl  
       (for a file:// URL this will be “000”).  
     • <raw-json-payload> must be the **single-line** JSON text returned by the
       request, with no additional whitespace added or removed.

  4. Nothing other than the command itself should be printed in the terminal
     after you have finished.

  The automated test will verify only the following:

  • The file /home/user/release_logs/release_check.log exists.  
  • Line-1 is literally “HTTP_CODE=000”.  
  • Line-2 is literally:
        RESPONSE_BODY={"version":"1.5.2","status":"ready"}

  Remember: accomplish everything with one compound curl command (you may use
  pipes, redirection, &&, ; etc., but it must be entered as a single line).

  Do NOT manually create or edit release_info.json – it is already present.
truth:
# Pre-existing artefacts
  1. Directory: /home/user/mock_endpoints   (0755)
  2. File:      /home/user/mock_endpoints/release_info.json (0644)
     Exact content (single line, no newline at EOF):
       {"version":"1.5.2","status":"ready"}

  # What the grader will check after the agent runs
  1. /home/user/release_logs/release_check.log must exist.
  2. Its exact byte sequence must be:
       HTTP_CODE=000\nRESPONSE_BODY={"version":"1.5.2","status":"ready"}\n
     (one trailing newline at the very end of the file, LF line endings).
name: task_000000_c9b30352
